channel.title: The Katipunan on Maharlikanism
channel.description: Recent content in The Katipunan on Maharlikanism

item.title: Denouncement of the Conspiracy and its Discoverer
item.description: Teodoro Patiño. A name which all Spaniards should pronounce with pleasure, because, by his repentance, inspired by divine Providence (96), Spain was saved from an unending series of bitter experiences.

item.title: Freemasonry
item.description:
Freemasonry has been the principal factor for the development in these islands for:

  • anti-religious ideas
  • secret societies

Some 20 years ago, there was installed in this country, a lodge dependent upon the Gr∴ Or∴ Español (7).

item.title: Kataastaasang Kalagayan Katipunan Nang Mang̃a Anak Nang Bayan
item.description:
Supreme Society of the Sons of the People1.

Whilst Rizal, in Manila, was engaged in the organization of the “Liga Filipina” into which only the well-to-do or educated classes could enter (70), an attempt [38]which, for that time, failed on account of his immediate deportation, Marcelo H. del Pilar, from Madrid, in July 1892, advised the creation of another association, which was to be similar thereto, but which was to include the agricultural laborers and persons of little or no education and instruction (71), but who directed in the localities by the caciques and chiefs, were to form an enormous nucleus which should, at the proper time, give forth the cry of rebellion. He (Pilar) provided minute instructions concerning the organization and forwarded a project of regulations.

item.title: La Liga Filipina
item.description: Rizal was pardoned by His Excellency the Captain General of the Archipelago, D. Eulogio Despujol (42), after the making of a thousand and one lying protests of repentance, reached this capital in May 1892, being received by his countrymen with extraordinary proofs of enthusiasm and rejoicing; and converting himself into an apostle of filibusterism, commenced a campaign of scandalous propaganda.

item.title: The Katipunan
item.description:
Appendix A. A∴ L∴ G∴ D∴ A∴ M∴

G∴ R∴ Log∴ SUNT∴

“The executive Commission sends to the V. Masters D. Deg. O. O. T. and O. G. O. S. of the L. Log. of the Obedience.

item.title: The Katipunan
item.description:
The Katipunan: The Rise and Fall of the Filipino Commune

Author: J. Brecknock Watson (AKA Francis St. Clair)

Triangular logo of the Katipunan with the letters K. K. K. Z. Ll. B.

item.title: The Katipunan
item.description:
To the lodge Nilad, the Gr∴ Sec∴ of the Gr∴ Or∴ Esp∴ wrote from Madrid, June 8th 1892:

AL∴ G∴ D∴ G∴ A∴ D∴ U∴ Liberty.—Equality.—Fraternity. Universal Freemasonry. Spanish Family.

item.title: The Propaganda Movement
item.description: At the end of the year 1888, Marcelo del Pilar, a lawyer of Bulacán, and a frenetic filibuster, considering himself in peril of deportation in consequence of juridical proceedings formed against him.
